title: Price to Earnings Ratio
snippet: The P/E ratio compares a company's share price to its earnings per share and is commonly used for valuation.

title: Market Capitalization
snippet: Market capitalization equals share price multiplied by total outstanding shares.

title: Return on Equity
snippet: ROE measures how efficiently a company generates profit from shareholder equity.

title: Debt to Equity Ratio
snippet: The debt-to-equity ratio evaluates a company's financial leverage and capital structure.

title: Free Cash Flow
snippet: Free cash flow represents cash remaining after operating expenses and capital expenditures.

title: Dividend Yield
snippet: Dividend yield measures annual dividend payments relative to the stock price.

title: Bull Market
snippet: A bull market is characterized by rising stock prices and optimistic investor sentiment.

title: Bear Market
snippet: A bear market refers to prolonged declines in stock prices and negative sentiment.

title: Support and Resistance
snippet: Support and resistance levels help traders identify potential price reversal zones.

title: Moving Average
snippet: A moving average smooths price data to identify trends and momentum.

title: RSI Indicator
snippet: The Relative Strength Index measures momentum and helps identify overbought or oversold conditions.

title: MACD Indicator
snippet: MACD tracks trend strength using moving average convergence and divergence.

title: Candlestick Patterns
snippet: Candlestick charts visualize price action and market psychology over time.

title: Volume Analysis
snippet: Trading volume helps confirm price trends and breakout movements.

title: NIFTY 50 Index
snippet: The NIFTY 50 tracks the performance of 50 major companies listed on NSE.

title: SENSEX Index
snippet: The SENSEX tracks 30 large companies listed on BSE.

title: Bank Nifty
snippet: Bank Nifty measures the performance of major Indian banking stocks.

title: Large Cap Stocks
snippet: Large-cap companies typically have stable earnings and significant market capitalization.

title: Mid Cap Stocks
snippet: Mid-cap companies often provide a balance between growth and stability.

title: Small Cap Stocks
snippet: Small-cap stocks generally offer higher growth potential but greater risk.

title: Mutual Funds
snippet: Mutual funds pool money from investors and invest in diversified portfolios.

title: Index Funds
snippet: Index funds aim to replicate the performance of a benchmark index.

title: Exchange Traded Funds
snippet: ETFs trade like stocks while tracking an index, commodity, or basket of assets.

title: Systematic Investment Plan
snippet: A SIP allows investors to invest fixed amounts regularly into mutual funds.

title: Asset Allocation
snippet: Asset allocation distributes investments across asset classes to manage risk.

title: Portfolio Diversification
snippet: Diversification reduces risk by spreading investments across different assets.

title: Risk Reward Ratio
snippet: The risk-reward ratio compares potential profit against potential loss.

title: Stop Loss Order
snippet: A stop-loss order automatically exits a position when a predefined price is reached.

title: Options Trading
snippet: Options provide the right but not the obligation to buy or sell an asset at a specified price.

title: Futures Contracts
snippet: Futures contracts obligate buyers and sellers to transact at a future date and price.

title: Call Option
snippet: A call option gives the holder the right to buy an underlying asset.

title: Put Option
snippet: A put option gives the holder the right to sell an underlying asset.

title: Open Interest
snippet: Open interest measures the number of outstanding derivative contracts.

title: India VIX
snippet: India VIX estimates expected market volatility over the next 30 days.

title: Inflation Impact
snippet: Higher inflation can reduce purchasing power and affect equity valuations.

title: Interest Rates
snippet: Interest rate changes influence borrowing costs and market liquidity.

title: GDP Growth
snippet: GDP growth reflects the expansion of economic activity in a country.

title: Foreign Institutional Investors
snippet: FII flows can significantly impact Indian equity markets.

title: Domestic Institutional Investors
snippet: DII activity often offsets foreign investment flows.

title: Retail Investors
snippet: Retail investors participate in markets using personal capital.

title: Zerodha Kite Platform
snippet: Kite is Zerodha's trading platform for stocks, derivatives, and mutual funds.

title: Sharekhan Trading Platform
snippet: Sharekhan provides brokerage and investment services for Indian investors.

title: Earnings Per Share
snippet: EPS measures a company's profitability on a per-share basis.

title: Book Value Per Share
snippet: Book value per share represents net asset value attributable to shareholders.

title: Intrinsic Value
snippet: Intrinsic value estimates the true worth of a company based on fundamentals.

title: Discounted Cash Flow
snippet: DCF valuation estimates business value using future cash flows discounted to present value.

title: Economic Moat
snippet: An economic moat is a sustainable competitive advantage protecting long-term profitability.

title: Sector Rotation
snippet: Investors often shift capital between sectors based on economic cycles.

title: Momentum Investing
snippet: Momentum investing focuses on stocks with strong recent performance.

title: Value Investing
snippet: Value investing seeks undervalued companies trading below intrinsic value.
